subject: Sports
title: The Olympic Torch Relay
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Olympic Torch Relay.

hook.text: Thousands of runners carry a flame across continents to open each Olympic Games.

buildup.text: The torch is lit in Olympia, Greece, using a parabolic mirror and sunlight—just like the ancients did.

discovery.text: The modern relay began at Berlin 1936. Over 3,000 runners carried the flame across seven countries.

twist.text: Berlin 1936 was Hitler's Games, making the relay's origin deeply controversial despite its beauty.

climax.text: Today the relay visits dozens of nations, symbolizing peace and unity before every Games.

punchline.text: A flame from ancient Greece opens every modern Olympics.

quiz.question: Where is the Olympic torch lit before each Games?
